name: Stir-Fried Bean Curd with Ground Pork and Oyster Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1/2 teaspoon finely minced hot green chile
1 pound firm bean curd, cut into 3/4-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix together the ground pork, egg, 1 tablespoon cornstarch, five-spice powder, scallions, sugar, sesame oil, and salt.
2. Heat vegetable oil in a hot wok over high heat. Add small clumps of the pork mixture and stir-fry for about 1 minute, or until browned and cooked through. Remove from wok and set aside.
3. Lower the heat to medium. Add the remaining scallions, red pepper, green chile, garlic, and ginger root to the wok. Stir-fry for 30 seconds.
4. Add the browned pork back into the wok and stir well with the remaining sugar.
5. Turn the heat high and add chicken stock, soy sauce, and oyster sauce. Stir well and bring to a boil.
6. Mix 2 teaspoons cornstarch with cold water in a cup, then stir into the wok. Gently stir in the bean curd cubes, being careful not to break them.
7. Remove from wok and place on serving platter. Sprinkle with the remaining sesame oil.
Yield: 4 servings
